verses.text: The people complain; fire from the LORD burns the edge of the camp. Taberah. Murmuring kindles God's anger.
The people complain; fire from the LORD burns the edge of the camp. Taberah. Murmuring kindles God's anger.
The people complain; fire from the LORD burns the edge of the camp. Taberah. Murmuring kindles God's anger.
The mixt multitude lust for flesh, despising the manna. Lust. They scorn God's daily bread.
The mixt multitude lust for flesh, despising the manna. Lust. They scorn God's daily bread.
The mixt multitude lust for flesh, despising the manna. Lust. They scorn God's daily bread.
The manna is described, sweet and freely given. Manna. God's provision was truly good.
The manna is described, sweet and freely given. Manna. God's provision was truly good.
The manna is described, sweet and freely given. Manna. God's provision was truly good.
Moses complains he cannot bear all this people alone. Burdened. The leader cries under the load.
Moses complains he cannot bear all this people alone. Burdened. The leader cries under the load.
Moses complains he cannot bear all this people alone. Burdened. The leader cries under the load.
He begs God to kill him rather than bear it. Overwhelmed. The weight feels too great.
He begs God to kill him rather than bear it. Overwhelmed. The weight feels too great.
He begs God to kill him rather than bear it. Overwhelmed. The weight feels too great.
God bids him gather seventy elders to share the Spirit. Helpers. God spreads the burden.
God bids him gather seventy elders to share the Spirit. Helpers. God spreads the burden.
God will give flesh a whole month, till it loathes them. Flesh. Lust is answered with surfeit.
God will give flesh a whole month, till it loathes them. Flesh. Lust is answered with surfeit.
God will give flesh a whole month, till it loathes them. Flesh. Lust is answered with surfeit.
Moses doubts; God asks, 'Is the LORD's hand waxed short?' God's power. Nothing is too hard for Him.
Moses doubts; God asks, 'Is the LORD's hand waxed short?' God's power. Nothing is too hard for Him.
Moses doubts; God asks, 'Is the LORD's hand waxed short?' God's power. Nothing is too hard for Him.
The Spirit rests on the seventy, and they prophesy. The Spirit. God equips the chosen.
The Spirit rests on the seventy, and they prophesy. The Spirit. God equips the chosen.
Eldad and Medad prophesy in the camp. In the camp. The Spirit is not confined.
Eldad and Medad prophesy in the camp. In the camp. The Spirit is not confined.
Joshua would forbid them, but Moses wishes all were prophets. Generous. Moses envies no one God's gifts.
Joshua would forbid them, but Moses wishes all were prophets. Generous. Moses envies no one God's gifts.
Joshua would forbid them, but Moses wishes all were prophets. Generous. Moses envies no one God's gifts.
Quails are blown in; the people gather them greedily. Quails. God sends flesh in abundance.
Quails are blown in; the people gather them greedily. Quails. God sends flesh in abundance.
While the flesh is in their mouths, a plague smites them. Judgment. Greed brings death at Kibroth-hattaavah.
While the flesh is in their mouths, a plague smites them. Judgment. Greed brings death at Kibroth-hattaavah.
While the flesh is in their mouths, a plague smites them. Judgment. Greed brings death at Kibroth-hattaavah.
